title: Jx Document
description: Schema for Jx component files. A Jx document is a JSON object that declaratively describes a reactive web component: its structure (DOM tree), styling, type definitions ($defs), runtime state, and inline or external functions. Reactivity is powered by @vue/reactivity.

$schema.description: URI identifying the Jx dialect version. Enables schema-aware IDE tooling.

$id.description: Component identifier string. Used by tooling and the builder.

$defs.description: Pure JSON Schema type definitions for this component. All entries are reusable type schemas — no runtime artifacts are produced. Referenced from state entries via $ref. Naming convention: PascalCase.

state.description: Runtime variables for this component. All entries are reactive by default. Entry shape is determined by value type: scalar/array → reactive property, string with ${} → computed, object with $prototype → function or data source, object with type and default → typed reactive property.

$media.description: Named media breakpoints following CSS @custom-media convention. Keys use the CSS custom property -- prefix.

$elements.description: Custom element dependencies. Items are either $ref objects pointing to JX element definitions, or bare npm package name strings for web component libraries.

oneOf.description: npm package specifier (must declare customElements in package.json)

$head.description: Page-level <head> entries. Array of element definitions for meta tags, link tags, script tags, etc. Merged with layout and site-level $head entries.

$layout.description: Layout reference for pages. String path to a layout JSON file, or false to opt out of the default layout.

$paths.description: Dynamic route parameters. Maps parameter names to data sources for generating one page per entry at build time.

title.description: Page title. Can be a static string or a template string with ${} expressions.

imports.description: Import map: $prototype names to .class.json file paths. Allows state entries to reference external classes by name without $src.

observedAttributes.description: HTML attributes the custom element watches for changes. Follows the Web Components observedAttributes convention.

cases.description: Switch cases object. Maps case values to element definitions or external component refs. Used alongside $switch for dynamic component rendering.

DefsMap.description: Map of reusable JSON Schema type definitions. Keys are PascalCase type names. No runtime artifacts are produced.

TypeDefEntry.description: A $defs type definition entry. Must be a pure JSON Schema type definition or a class definition (.class.json format).

StateMap.description: Map of runtime variables. Keys are camelCase (public) or #-prefixed (private). All entries are reactive by default.

StateEntry.description: A single state entry. Shape is determined by value type: scalar/array → naked reactive property, string with ${} → computed, object with $prototype: "Function" → function, object with $prototype: <other> → data source, object with type and default → typed reactive property, plain object → naked object reactive property.

TypedStateDef.description: A typed reactive state variable with explicit type and default value. The type property is a JSON Schema or $ref to a $defs type definition. The default property is the initial runtime value.

type.description: JSON Schema type definition, $ref to a $defs type, or JSON Schema type string.

attribute.description: Linked HTML attribute name for CEM extraction.

reflects.description: Whether property changes reflect back to the HTML attribute.

$ref.description: Reference to a shared type definition.

PureTypeDef.description: A reusable JSON Schema type definition for tooling only. No function, no runtime artifact. Referenced from state entries via $ref. Naming convention: PascalCase.

FunctionDef.description: A function declaration. $prototype must be "Function". body (inline) and $src (external) are mutually exclusive. First parameter is always state (the reactive scope).

body.description: Inline function body string. First implicit parameter is state.
examples: state.items.push({ id: Date.now(), text: "", done: false })

parameters.description: Function parameters (after the implicit state parameter). Accepts CEM-compatible parameter objects or bare string names for backward compatibility.

name.description: Explicit function name. Defaults to the state key name.

$src.description: External module specifier. Mutually exclusive with body.

$export.description: Named export in $src module. Defaults to the state key name.

type.description: Return type for tooling (JSON Schema or CEM { text } format).

emits.description: Array of CEM-compatible Event objects this function dispatches. Used for CEM extraction and studio event discovery.

ClassDef.description: A .class.json schema-defined class. $prototype must be "Class". Defines fields, constructor, methods, and type parameters via $defs. Optionally points to a JS module via $implementation for hybrid execution.

title.description: PascalCase class name, used as the export name.

extends.description: Base class — string name or $ref to another .class.json.

$implementation.description: Relative path to a JS module containing the actual class implementation.

$defs.description: Class members: parameters, returnTypes, fields, constructor, methods.

parameters.description: Reusable typed parameter schemas, keyed by name.

returnTypes.description: Output type schemas, keyed by name.

fields.description: Class fields with role, access, scope, and type information.

ClassParameterDef.description: A typed parameter definition for a class.

format.description: When "json-schema", this parameter's value is itself a JSON Schema.

ClassFieldDef.description: A class field definition with access control and scope.

$prototype.description: Data source prototype for this field (e.g., "Request").

ClassMethodDef.description: A class method or accessor definition.

ExternalClassDef.description: An external class / data source. $prototype is a constructor name (not "Function"). When $prototype is not in the built-in registry, $src is required. All state entries are reactive by default.

$prototype.description: Constructor name — built-in Web API class or external class name.

$src.description: External module specifier. Required when $prototype is not a built-in.

$export.description: Named export in $src module. Defaults to the $prototype value.

src.description: Configuration property passed to external class constructor.

ElementDef.description: A Jx element definition. Maps directly to a DOM element.

ChildrenValue.description: Static array of child definitions, or an Array namespace for dynamic lists.

ArrayNamespace.description: Dynamic mapped list. Re-renders when the items state entry changes.

SwitchDef.description: Reactive $ref that drives which case to render.

StyleObject.description: CSS style definition. camelCase property names follow CSSOM convention. Keys starting with :, ., &, or [ are treated as nested CSS selectors. Keys matching $media breakpoint names are treated as responsive rules.

oneOf.description: Nested CSS selector or media breakpoint rules.

AttributesObject.description: HTML attributes and ARIA attributes set via element.setAttribute().

PropsObject.description: Explicit prop passing at a component boundary.

RefObject.description: A $ref binding. Resolves to a state entry (reactive) or plain value (static).

InternalRef.description: Reference to a $defs type definition in the current component.

StateRef.description: Reference to a state entry (runtime variable) in the current component.

ExternalRef.description: Reference to an external Jx component file.

GlobalRef.description: Reference to a window or document global.

ParentRef.description: Reference to a named state entry passed via $props from a parent component.

MapRef.description: Reference to the current Array map iteration context.

CemParameter.description: A CEM-compatible parameter definition for a function. Follows the Custom Elements Manifest Parameter shape.

type.description: Parameter type (JSON Schema or CEM { text } format).

CemEvent.description: A CEM-compatible event definition. Describes a CustomEvent the function dispatches.

TagName.description: HTML element tag name or custom element name (must contain a hyphen per Web Components spec).
